content:
Faster APIs are rarely about bigger servers. Most of the time, they’re about sending less data and doing less work.

5 min read
7 hours ago

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

How to Optimize JSON Responses in Next.js APIs for Faster LoadsA few months ago, I was investigating why a dashboard felt sluggish despite having decent backend performance.
Database queries were fast. Server response times looked fine. Infrastructure wasn’t under pressure.
The real culprit was surprisingly simple.
Our API endpoint was returning nearly 300 KB of JSON when the frontend only needed about 40 KB.
Every request was carrying a truckload of unnecessary data across the network, only for the browser to parse it and ignore most of it.
Once we trimmed the payload and optimized the response structure, the dashboard became noticeably faster without changing a single server instance.
If you’re building APIs with Next.js, optimizing JSON responses is one of the easiest performance wins you can get.
Why Large JSON Responses Hurt Performance
Developers often focus on database performance and caching, which are important.
